mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts signals a strengthening demand for housing, which in turn drives economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in payroll employment and decrease in unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market, which in turn drives economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals a strengthening supply chain, which in turn drives economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of the real personal consumption expenditures above 4% YoY growth signals a strengthening demand, which in turn drives economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in federal funds rate and decrease in consumer price index signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decrease in unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market, which in turn drives economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in GDP signals a strengthening demand, which in turn drives economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
